# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 84

This is turn 84. Turns 1 through 83 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 84.

## Turn 84 — the scalar radical is Frattini, closing the surjective-fibre gap

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit. Proof-level occurrences of the displayed exponent mean the genuine profinite idempotent \(\omega _2\); \(40491355905\) is its required residue modulo \(85667662080\). 

Turn 83 established, for the critical block,
\[
R=\Phi(K),\qquad M=K/R,\qquad
T_0=(K\cap S)/R=\operatorname{rad}_{\mathbf F_2[G/K]}(M),
\]
with simple nontrivial head \(M/T_0\simeq V\). It also reduced every nonzero invariant scalar pushout of \(R\) to a nonsingular determinant problem on \(V\). 

There was still one implicit surjectivity issue. A nonzero filtration-one edge gives exactly half of the **relation-compatible** \(T_0\)-lifts, but the Fourier quantity \(m_{\Gamma,\lambda}\) counts **surjective** maps to \(B=G/R\). A priori, removing nonsurjective \(T_0\)-lifts could destroy the half-half ratio.

The following general lemma removes that issue uniformly.

### Radical–Frattini lemma

Let \(B\) be a finite group and let \(M\triangleleft B\) be elementary abelian. Regard \(M\) as an \(\mathbf F_p[B/M]\)-module. Then
\[
\operatorname{rad}_{\mathbf F_p[B/M]}(M)\leq \Phi(B).
\tag{84.1}
\]

**Proof.** Let \(U<B\) be maximal.

If \(M\leq U\), the inclusion is immediate.

Otherwise \(UM=B\), so \(U\to B/M\) is surjective. Put \(X=U\cap M\). Then \(X\) is a \(B/M\)-submodule of \(M\). It is maximal: if
\[
X<Y<M
\]
were a \(B/M\)-submodule, then \(UY\) would be a subgroup satisfying
\[
U<UY<B,
\]
contradicting maximality of \(U\). Hence every maximal subgroup not containing \(M\) intersects \(M\) in a maximal \(B/M\)-submodule.

Therefore \(\operatorname{rad}(M)\) lies in every maximal subgroup of \(B\), proving (84.1). \(\square\)

Equality need not hold; only the inclusion is required.

### Application to the critical block

Put
\[
B=G/R,\qquad M=K/R,\qquad T_0=\operatorname{rad}(M).
\]
The lemma gives
\[
T_0\leq \Phi(B).
\tag{84.2}
\]

Consequently, if
\[
\bar\rho:\Gamma\twoheadrightarrow B/T_0
\]
is surjective, then **every** lift \(\rho:\Gamma\to B\) is automatically surjective. Indeed, for \(J=\rho(\Gamma)\),
\[
JT_0=B,
\]
and \(T_0\leq\Phi(B)\) forces \(J=B\).

This is exactly the missing bridge between deformation-theoretic lift counts and surjection counts.

Suppose now that the scalar pushout class associated with
\[
0\neq\lambda\in (R^\vee)^B
\]
has a nonzero filtration-one edge across \(T_0\). Its pullback along every surjection to \(B/T_0\) is nonzero. Perfect deformation-complex duality therefore makes its obstruction a nonzero affine functional on the \(T_0\)-lift torsor. Turn 82 supplies that perfect duality and its uniform lift multiplicity for arbitrary elementary, possibly unipotent modules. 

Hence exactly half of the \(T_0\)-lifts survive. By (84.2), all these lifts are surjective, so no Möbius subtraction changes the ratio:
\[
m_{\Gamma,\lambda}=\frac{|X_\Gamma|}{2}
\qquad
\text{whenever the \(T_0\)-edge is nonzero.}
\tag{84.3}
\]

Scalar decorations do not cut this torsor, since Turn 83 proved that every elementary scalar decoration annihilates all of \(K\), and therefore \(T_0\).

If the \(T_0\)-edge vanishes, the scalar class inflates through \(B/T_0\). Its restriction to
\[
V=M/T_0
\]
is the nonsingular determinant form supplied by the simple-head lemma. The transgression then forces the \(V\)-extension to split; shearing removes any remaining mixed edge, and the Wall–Arf/local deep-unit comparison gives the same determinant zero count on both sources.

Thus every nonzero Fourier character has one of two completely uniform behaviours:

\[
m_{\Gamma,\lambda}
=
\begin{cases}
|X_\Gamma|/2,
   &\text{nonzero \(T_0\)-edge},\\[2mm]
\text{matched split-determinant expression},
   &\text{zero \(T_0\)-edge}.
\end{cases}
\tag{84.4}
\]

There is no residual dependence on the distribution of nonsurjective \(T_0\)-lifts.

### Fixed-tame-frame induction, now fully well-founded

Fix a finite decorated marked target \((G,L,\theta)\), a decoration value \(\psi\), and a full tame frame
\[
\alpha:T_{\mathrm{tame}}\twoheadrightarrow G/L.
\]

For
\[
\Gamma\in\{\Gamma_{A_2},G_{\mathbf Q_2}\},
\]
write
\[
N_\Gamma(G,L;\alpha,\theta,\psi)
\]
for the corresponding number of surjections.

Induct on \(|L|\).

If every \(G\)-chief factor of \(L\) is trivial, the terminal scalar theorem identifies \(G\) with a fibre product of its tame quotient and a finite \(2\)-group. The count is therefore determined by the common maximal pro-\(2\) quotient.

Otherwise choose the first nontrivial factor \(P/S\simeq V\) and the minimal active subgroup \(K\) from Turn 83.

First lift from \(G/K\) to \(G/R\) through
\[
M=K/R.
\]
Since
\[
(M^\vee)^{G/K}=0,
\]
finite-Heisenberg deformation duality gives zero obstruction and exactly
\[
2^{\,2\dim M}
\]
relation-compatible lifts over every generated lower map. Proper-image contributions have strictly smaller marked wild kernel and retain the same tame frame, so induction subtracts them.

Next lift through \(R\). Put
\[
D=(R^\vee)^{G/R}.
\]
For the \(R\)-valued obstruction \(o_\Gamma(\rho)\), Fourier inversion gives
\[
\#\{\rho:o_\Gamma(\rho)=0\}
=
\frac1{|D|}
\sum_{\lambda\in D}
\bigl(2m_{\Gamma,\lambda}-|X_\Gamma|\bigr).
\tag{84.5}
\]

For \(\lambda=0\), equality follows from the already-matched \(G/R\)-count. For \(\lambda\neq0\), equality follows from (84.4). Hence every Fourier summand agrees.

Once the \(R\)-obstruction vanishes, Turn 82 gives the same lift multiplicity. Every such lift is surjective: if \(J<G\) maps onto \(G/R\), then
\[
(J\cap K)R=K,
\]
and \(R=\Phi(K)\) forces \(J\cap K=K\), hence \(J=G\).

Therefore
\[
N_{\Gamma_{A_2}}(G,L;\alpha,\theta,\psi)
=
N_{G_{\mathbf Q_2}}(G,L;\alpha,\theta,\psi)
\tag{84.6}
\]
for every fixed tame frame.

Summing over \(\alpha\), then taking trivial decoration and
\[
L=O_2(G),
\]
gives the official finite-target semantics:
\[
|\operatorname{Sur}(\Gamma_{A_2},G)|
=
|\operatorname{Sur}(G_{\mathbf Q_2},G)|
\]
for every finite \(G\), subject to the established lemmas. Dividing by \(|\operatorname{Aut}(G)|\) gives the verifier quantity.

**ESTABLISHED**: Proved the general radical–Frattini inclusion
\[
\operatorname{rad}_{\mathbf F_p[B/M]}(M)\leq\Phi(B)
\]
for every normal elementary abelian \(M\triangleleft B\). Applied it to the critical block to show \(T_0\leq\Phi(G/R)\). Consequently every \(T_0\)-lift of a surjection is automatically surjective, so a nonzero filtration-one edge remains exactly half-split after imposing generation. This closes the previously implicit surjective-fibre gap and makes the fixed-tame-frame induction a genuinely well-founded recurrence.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun. A publication-grade proof must still state the exact Thompson critical-subgroup theorem being invoked and consolidate the preceding determinant and finite-Heisenberg lemmas, but no finite-quotient branch is currently left open.

**NEXT**: Audit and state the precise Thompson critical-subgroup input as a standalone proposition, then assemble the complete fixed-frame theorem and unchanged verifier submission.

[BREAKTHROUGH]
